Rous, Thomas, s. J., of Wootton-Bridge (under Edge), co. Gloucester, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 21 Feb., 1690-1, aged 15; brother of John 1690.
King, William, s. Rog., of Bristol, Somerset, paup. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 5 March, 1690-1, aged 16; B.A. 19 Feb., 1694-5.
Goodenough, William, s. Tho., of Lechlade, co. Gloucester, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 30 May, 1690, aged 17.
Colbatch, Joseph, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 15 Dec., 1690; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 1694.

Cane, Joseph, s. Henry, of Little Hillingdon, Middlesex, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 12 March, 1691-2, aged 17; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 5 Dec., 1695, M.A. 17 Nov., 1698, vicar of Shiplake, Oxon, 1710, rector of Ludgarshall, Bucks, 1730. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Marchant, Francis, s. James, of Kilmersdon, Somerset, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 1 July, 1691, aged 18; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 1695, vicar of Alderminster, co. Worcester, 1698. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Janns, George, s. G., of Ruckens, Kent, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 27 April, 1691, aged 16; B.A. 7 March, 1694-5, M.A. 1698.
Gomeldon, William, s. Th., of Sellinge, Kent, arm. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 27 April, 1691, aged 16.
Twigden, Nicholas, s. Benjamin, of Milton, Northants, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 17 July, 1692, aged 17, B.A. 1696; rector of Middleton Malsor, Northants, 1724-45. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hurdis, Thomas, s. Th., of London, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 14 June, 1692, aged 17. bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1698. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Cooke, John, s. J., of Hannay, Berks, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1692, aged 17; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 1696, M.A. 1699.
Vane, Gilbert, s. Christopher, gent. (arm., of London; on fly leaf). St. Edmund Hall, matric. 27 Oct., 1692, aged 16.
Bruch, John, s. Mat., of Warrington, co. Lancaster, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 27 Jan., 1692-3, aged 16.
Jones, Thomas, s. Tho., pp. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 7 March, 1692-3, aged 18.

Abell, Joseph, s. Hump, of St. Dunstan's, London, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 22 March, 1693-4, aged 17; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 1697, fellow Merton Coll. 1700, M.A. 1702, rector of Farleigh, Surrey, 1707, vicar of Chelsham and Warlingham, Surrey, 1715. See Hearne, i. 117; Rawlinson, xx. 7; & Foster's Index Ecc.

St. Barbe, Francis, s. Fran., of Salisbury, Wilts, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 3 July, 1693, aged 16, B.A. 1697, M.A. 18 March, 1699-1700; vicar of Mylor, Cornwall, 1701, and of Mabe 1735; brother of the next-named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Pelham, Thomas, , y.s. Nic., of Catsfeild, Sussex, equitis. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 3 July, 1693, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1696, M.P. Lewes in 9 parliaments 1705, until his death in 1743. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Loder, John, s. J., of Morton, co. Gloucester. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 24 March, 1693-4, aged 17; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 1698, M.A. 14 Feb., 1701-2, rector of Napton-on-the-Hill, co. Warwick, 1702. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hammond, James, s. Ja., cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 15 July, 1693, aged 18; B.A. 1697, M.A. 1700, rector of West Meston, Sussex, 1708. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Blundell, John (Blondell), s. Rob., of Norton, co. Worcester, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 4 May, 1693, aged 18; B.A. 16 March, 1696-7.
Foulkes, Robert, s. Charles, of Lockerley, Herts (rather Hants), gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 22 March, 1693-4, aged 17.
Goodricke, Henry, s. Joh., of Coventry, co. Warwick, arm. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 22 July, 1693, aged 16.
Alexander, Robert, s. Hen., of Binfield, Berks, arm. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 7 April, 1693, aged 19.
Bealing, Marm, s. Rich., of London, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 24 March, 1693-4, aged 17.

Acton, Whitmore, s. Edward, of Aldenham, Salop, baronet. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1694-5, aged 17; of Middle Temple 1698, 4th baronet, M.P. Bridgnorth 1710-13, died 9 Jan., 1731-2. See Foster's Peerage.
Dobbins, Henry (Dobyns), arm. St. Edmund Hall matric. 4 March, 1694-5; of Lincoln's Inn 1695, as 2nd son of William, a bencher of that society. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hickes, Joseph (Heekes), s. William, of "Aishbourne Underhill," co. Gloucester, pp. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 14 Feb., 1694-5, aged 18.
March, Humphrey, s. Joh., of Newcastle (city), cler. St. Edmund Hall., matric. 16 March, 1694-5, aged 16; B.A. 1698, M.A. 1701.
Platt, Francis, s. William, of Swallowfield, Berks, p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 13 March, 1694-5, aged 18.
Burton, Robert, arm. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 4 March, 1694-5. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Wanley, Humphrey, s. Nath(aniel), of Coventry (city), cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 7 May, 1695, aged 23, "antiquary and palæographer," born at Coventry 21 March, 1671-2, apprenticed to a draper in Coventry, assistant in the Bodleian 1696-1700, secretary of the society for propagating christian knowledge 1702-8, library keeper to Robert Harley 1700 (earl of Oxford 1711) and to his son Edward, 2nd earl of Oxford, whose celebrated collection the Harleian MSS. are in the British museum; died 6 July, 1726, buried in St. Marylebone church. See Rawl. i. 272b; & Genealogist, N.S. i. (1884), 114, 178, 256.

Hearne, Thomas, s. George, of White Waltham, Berks, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 5 Dec., 1695, aged 15; B.A. 1699, M.A. 1703; born at White Waltham 11 July, 1679 (his father parish clerk there), assistant keeper Bodleian library about 1701, author of "Remarks and Collections," "Reliquiæ Bodleianæ," esquire bedel of civil law 1715, born at Littlefields in the parish of White Waltham 11 July, 1679, died in Edmund Hall 10 June, 1735, buried 14th in St. Peter's churchyard; will at Oxford proved 3 July, 1735. See O.H.S. ii. vii. xiii.; Rawl, ii. 145, xvii. 226-248; & D.N.B.

Chambers, Cuthbert, s. Cut(hbert), of Ripon, Yorks, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 22 Oct., 1695, aged 17, B.A. 1699, M.A. 1702; fellow of Magdalen Coll. 1711, B.D., rector of Hurworth, co. Durham, 1712, preb. of Ripon 1706. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Hearne, i. 315.

Trefusis, Samuel, s. Fran., of Trefusis, Cornwall, arm. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 11 Oct., 1695, aged 16; of Trefusis, baptised 6 Oct., 1676, M.P. Penryn in 9 parliaments, 1698-1713 and March, 1713-14 to 1722; buried at Mylor 4 April, 1724. See Foster's Peerage, B. Clinton.
Butler, Richard, s. Ab., of Audley, co. Stafford, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 9 March, 1695-6, aged 16; B.A. 1699, an officer in the revenue, died at Knutsford, Cheshire. See Rawlinson, v. 134.
EdenEdward, s. Edw., of "Oxil," co. Warwick, pp. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 7 May, 1695, aged 20; B.A. from Gloucester Hall 6 March, 1698-9, M.A. 1702.
Oglander, (Sir) William, s. John, of Isle of Wight, bart. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 11 April, 1695, aged 15; 3rd bart., died Aug. 1734.
Walker, John, s. Joh., of Piddington, Oxon, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 23 Jan., 1695-6, aged 17, B.A. 1699, M.A. 1701.
Albert, Joseph, s. Joh., of Worcester (city), p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 9 March, 1695-6, aged 19.
Hall, William, s. Joh., of Bromyard, co. Hereford, pp. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 7 May, 1695, aged 16.
Nason, John, p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 7 May, 1695, B.A. 7 March, 1698-9.

Felton, Henry, s. John, of London, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 31 March, 1696, aged 16; B.A. 1699, M.A. 1702; B.D. from Queen's Coll., 1709, D.D. 1712; principal of Edmund Hall 1722-40, rector of Whitwell, co. Derby, 1712, chaplain to Duke of Rutland, rector of Barwick in Elmet, Yorks, 1736, until his death 1 March, 1739-40. See Rawl. i. 325 (West. Abbey, 298, for his father's burial); Hearne, i. 270, iii. 387; & D.N.B.
Bilson, Thomas, s. Thomas, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 12 Nov., 1696, aged 18.

Denton, Alexander, s. Alexander, of Hillesden, Bucks, arm. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 15 April, 1697, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1704, bencher 1720, K.C., serjt.-at-law 1722, attorney-general to the Duchy of Lancaster, recorder of Buckingham, M.P. Buckingham 1708 & 1715-22, chancellor to the Prince of Wales 1729, a judge of common pleas 1722, until his death 22 March, 1739-40, buried 30th at Hillesden, brother of Edmund 1695. See Foster's Judges and Barristers; Hearne, i. 154; & Rawlinson, i. 327, xx. 177.

Newdegate, Richard, s. Tho., of Lewes, Sussex, arm. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 11 Oct., 1697, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1704, bencher 1733, M.P. Newark 1710-15; buried in the Temple church Saturday, 21 Dec., 1745. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Cole, Brian, s. Tho., of Onnebury, Salop, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 23 June, 1697, aged 18; vicar of Stottesdon, Salop, 1706, and of Bishops' Castle 1714. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Worsley, John, s. George, of isle of Wight, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1697, aged 18, B.A. 1701; rector of Gatcombe, I.W., 1708. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, James, s. Rice, of Disserth, co. Radnor, p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. March, 1697, aged 18; B.A. 1700.

Fox, Francis, s. F(rancis), of Brentford, Middlesex, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 14 April, 1698, aged 22; B.A. 1701, M.A. 1704, incorporated at Cambridge 1707, apprenticed to a mercer or a glover in London 6 years, chaplain to Sir Owen Buckingham, lord mayor of London, 1705, rector of Boscombe, Wilts, 1708, vicar of Potterne, Wilts, 1711, prebendary of Sarum 1717, rector of Woodford and Wilford 1717, vicar of St. Mary, Reading, 1726, rector of Stratton, Sarum, 1730; died 1738. See Hearne, i. 34; Rawl. iii. 286, xvii. 133; Foster's Index Eccl.; & D.N.B.

Kent, Abraham, s. John, of Woolverton, co. Warwick, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 11 March, 1698-9, aged 16; B.A. 1702, M.A. 1705, incorporated at Cambridge 1709, rector of Whitechurch, Kent, 1705, vicar of Great Amwell, Herts, 1713, rector of Netteswell, Essex, 1716. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Charlton, (Sir) Blunden, y.s. Francis, of Envil, co. Stafford, equitis. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 28 March, 1698, aged 16; 3rd baronet, died in Dec., 1742.

Cherry, Thomas, s. George, of Maidenhead, Berks, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 9 Feb., 1699-1700, aged 16; B.A. 1703, M.A. 1706, curate of Witney, Oxon, died 17 Nov., 1706, buried 20th in St. Andrews, Holborn. See Hearne, i. pp. 108-110.
Robinson, Joseph, s. J., of 'Bencoger,' co. Cambridge, p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 18 March, 1699-1700, aged 16; perhaps vicar of Burneston, Yorks, 1711. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Mount, James, s. Edw., of Pennington, co. Lancaster, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 24 Oct., 1699, aged 17; B.A. 1704.
Pugh, Hugh, s. James, of Preston, co. Radnor, p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 23 March, 1699-1700, aged 17.
Gardner, Edward, s. Tho., of London, pp. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 28 March, 1699, aged 19; B.A. 1702.
Winckley, John, s. Tho., of Preston, co. Lancaster, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 13 May, 1700, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1711. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Ridley, Giles, s. Nath., of Acton, co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 27 June, 1700, aged 16; B.A. 1704, M.A. 1708.

Spavan, John, s. Mart., of York (city), pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 31 May, 1701, aged 15, B.A. 22 Jan., 1704-5; M.A. from Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 1709; born at York 26 Dec., 1685, curate of St. Clement Danes, vicar of Great Maplestead, Essex, 1713-18; buried in the church 7 July, 1718. See Rawl. xix. 59; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Bennett, John, s. John, of London, Middlesex, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 31 May, 1701, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1708 (query a master in chancery 1717, until his death 4 Feb., 1738-9). See Rawlinson, vi. 126; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Sawyer, John, s. Edm(und), of Maidenhead, Berks, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 16 Jan., 1701-2, aged 16; buried at White Waltham in 1750.

Tindale, William, s. Walter, of Kingswood, Wilts, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 19 May, 1702, aged 15, B.A. 7 March, 1705-6, M.A. 1708; vicar of Elmstone-Hardwick 1712-21, rector of Coates 1721-63, vicar of St. Mary-de-Lode, Gloucester, 1722-62, rector of Charfield (all co. Gloucester), 1750-63. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Horler, Jeremiah, s. Jeremiah (4to.), of Yate, co. Gloucester, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 18 June, 1702, aged 15; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 1706; M.A. from Hart Hall 1709, rector of Little Sodbury, co. Gloucester, 1717. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Stapylton, (Sir) John, s. Brian, of Myton, Yorks, bart. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 12 June, 1702, aged 18; 3rd bart., M.P. Boroughbridge 1705-8; died 24 Oct., 1733. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Rogers, John, s. J(ohn), of Newcastle-upon-Tyne, arm. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 7 Dec., 1702, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1702. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Bickford, William, s. Arscot, of Dunsland, Devon, arm. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 28 March, 1702, aged 17; of Dunsland, brother of Edmund.
Mount, William, s. James, of Pennington, co. Lancaster, p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 3 Nov., 1702, aged 17; B.A. 29 Jan., 1706-7.

Willim, John, s. J., of Peterchurch, co. Hereford, gent. (subscribes "cler."). St. Edmund Hall, matric. 11 Feb., 1703-4, aged 17, B.A. 1707; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1728; one John Williams vicar of Peterchurch 1678, and rector of Bacton, (both) co. Hereford, 1680. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Evans, Morgan, s. M., of Cil-y-cwm, co. Carmarthen gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 9 Dec. 1703, aged 26; B. C. L. 1710, perhaps canon of Llandaff 1713, and chancellor 1718. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bickford, Edmund, s. 'Arscot,' of Bradford, Devon, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 8 June, 1703, aged 19 (17), bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1710. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Edwards, Robert, s. John, of Llanymynech, Salop, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 24 Nov., 1703, aged 21; his father rector of Llanymynech 1666. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Baylies, John, s. Philemon, of Alvechurch, co. Worcester, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 22 March, 1704-5, aged 16, B.A. 1708; M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1712, vicar of Snitterfield. co. Warwick, 1713. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Morland, Thomas, s. T., of Lasenby, Cumberland, p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 6 April, 1704, aged 18; B.A. 1707, vicar of Bapchild, Kent, 1709, and of Hartfield, Sussex, 1713. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Miles, Francis, s. Ric., of Burbage, co. Leicester, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 6 April, 1704, aged 18; B.A. 13 Feb., 1707-8, vicar of Norton-byGalby, co. Leicester, 1726. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Hull, William, s. Thomas, of Reading, Berks, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 17 Feb., 1704-5, aged 18, B.A. 1708; M.A. from Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, 1713; brother of John 1710.
Poole, German, s. Sam., of Radbourne, co. Derby, arm. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 13 Nov., 1704, aged 17 (subscribes as "Pole"); of Radbourne, high sheriff of co. Derby 1735; died 1765.
Cook, Nathaniel, s. John, of Newbery, Berks, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 17 Feb., 1704-5, aged 16; B.A. 1709, M.A. 1712 as Cooke.

Shelton, Joseph, s. John, of Salford, co. Warwick, p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 26 March, 1705, aged 23; student of Inner Temple 1696 (as son and heir of John, of West Bromwich, co. Stafford), vicar of Salford (Priors) 1721. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Harris, John, s. William, of Bredwardine, co. Hereford, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 14 March, 1705-6, aged 16; B.A. 2 March, 1709-10; brother of Francis 1696, etc.
Sherley, Thomas, s. Ralph, of Newbury, Berks, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 17 Dec., 1705, aged 17, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 1710; brother of Ralph 1700.
Ault, William (or Hult), s. G. (? 'Gul.') of Langford, co. Derby, p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 4 March, 1705-6, aged 18.
Herbert, John, s. Morgan, of Llanbedr, co. Cardigan, pp. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 5 July, 1705, aged 20.
Richards, George, s. James, of Oxford (city), p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 2 Dec., 1706, aged 18; B.A. 1710, vicar of Hadlow, Kent, 1717. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wells, Natha(niel), s. N., of Oxford (city), p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 4 March, 1706-7, aged 16, B.A. 22 Feb., 1710-11, M.A. 17 March, 1713-14.
Vincent, Henry, s. H., of Norwich (city), p.p. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 4 March, 1706-7, aged 20, B.A. 4 Feb., 1707-8.

King, Samuel, s. John, of Summerton, Oxon, pp. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 2 April, 1707, aged 18; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 1712, rector of King's Cliffe, Northants, 1714. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bromley, William, s. Francis, of Abberley, co. Worcester, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 2 April, 1707, aged 16; of Abberley, died in 1769, aged 70.
Smith, Thomas (Smyth), s. Isa., of Dursley, co. Gloucester, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 2 April, 1707, aged 18, B.A. 22 Feb., 1710-11.
Hatsell, Henry s. Laurence (4to.), of London, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 5 July, 1707, aged 15.

Loder, Seymour, s. Rob., of Lechlade, co. Gloucester, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 28 Feb., 1709-10, aged 17; B.A. 1713, M.A. 1716, rector of Hinton Waldrish, Berks, 1717, and of Kencott, Oxon, 1722. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Knapp, Coghill, s. John, of Blechinden, Oxon, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 15 April, 1709, aged 17; an attorney-at-law; died in Islington 23 Aug., 1743, and there buried. See Rawl. vi. 123.
Spry, William, s. Richard, of West Ogwell, Devon, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 4 June, 1709, aged 17, B.A. 13 Feb., 1712-13; brother of John 1705, and Richard 1703.
Senhouse, Wrightington, s. "Gul." (or "Gal."), of Eskrid, Yorks, paup. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 12 April, 1709, aged 17.
Greenewood, Thomas, s. Tho., of Chastleton, Oxon, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 7 July, 1709, aged 17.
Chalmers, Francis, 'cler. fil.' St. Edmund Hall, matric. 16 Dec., 1710; B.C.L. from New Inn Hall 18 March, 1712-13, M.A. Edinburgh University 20 Oct., 1709. See Hearne, iii. 325.
Purnell, Thomas, s. Joh., of Stinchcombe, co. Gloucester, paup. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 2 May, 1710, aged 18; B.A. 9 March, 1713-14.
Hall, Joseph, s. Sam., of Eardisley, co. Hereford, cler. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 18 Nov., 1710, aged 20.
Walker, John, s. J., of Westminster, Middx., gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 24 Oct.; 1710, aged 15.

Tillard, Joas, s. Abraham, of London, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 10 Oct., 1717, aged 16, B.A. 1711 (as Joash); bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1717; born 15 Jan., 1690, died Jan., 1774. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Thomas, Francis, s. Fran., of Newcastle, co. Glamorgan, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 17 July, 1712, aged 20.

Manning, Thomas, s. Francis, of Stinchcombe, co. Gloucester, paup. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 6 Nov., 1713, aged 14; B.A. 1717; perhaps vicar of Odiham with Grewell, Hants, 1731, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Cooke, William, s. Joh., of Bristol (city), gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 26 March, 1713, aged 17.

Berriman, John, s. Joh., of London, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 12 May, 1714, aged 19; B.A. 11 March, 1717-18, M.A. 1720, his father an apothecary, he was born in Bishopsgate Street, 27 Jan., 1690-1, and put apprentice to a wire-drawer in Foster Lane; lecturer of St. Mary Aldermary and St. Thomas Apostle 10 April, 1719, rector of New Timber, Sussex, 1732, vicar of St. Alban's, Wood Street, and St. Olave's, Silver Street, 1743, brother of William 1705. See Rawlinson, i. 288, and xvi. 182; & D.N.B.

Lewin, Edmund, s. John, of Broxbourne, Herts, gent. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 26 July, 1714, aged 16, B.A. 1718; M.A. from Magdalen Hall 31 Jan., 1720-1, B. and D.D. 1738, vicar of Standish, co. Gloucester, 1724, rector of Westmill, Herts, 1727, of Stone, Kent, 1754, chancellor of Rochester; died 24 July, 1771. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Chester's West. Abbey Reg. 50.
Powell, John, s. Joh., of St. James, Bristol, pleb. St. Edmund Hall, matric. 13 May, 1714, aged 19; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 1717.
